title: pouteria torta subsp. gallifructa
intro: Pouteria torta subsp. gallifructa, also known as the 'gallifruit tree', is a plant species within the family Sapotaceae, native to Mexico and Central America, and is often used for its medicinal and culinary purposes. The plant's bark and leaves are rich in alkaloids and saponins, which possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. The medicinal properties of Pouteria torta subsp. gallifructa have been reported to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
